name: Agent Session Service — continual conversation, host-resident
outcome_goal: A FastAPI service at 127.0.0.1:8421 exposes POST /agents, POST /agents/{id}/messages, GET /agents/{id}/messages, GET /agents/{id}/stream, DELETE /agents/{id}, GET /agents. Each message spawns `claude -p --resume <session_id> --output-format stream-json` with cwd=/Users/nate/Praxis. Session state persists under ~/.claude/projects/. Transcripts log to artifacts/agents/<agent_id>/messages.jsonl. A com.praxis.agent-sessions launchd plist with KeepAlive=true keeps the service alive. End-to-end smoke test: `curl -X POST localhost:8421/agents/{id}/messages -d '{"prompt":"what directory are you in"}'` returns a reply containing '/Users/nate/Praxis' within 60s.
anti_requirements: Do NOT sandbox the Claude subprocess — agent sessions must run as the user with full access to /Users/nate/Praxis, live Postgres, MCP servers, and the praxis CLI. That is the entire point.
Do NOT invent new transcript or session storage — Claude Code already persists sessions under ~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-cwd>/<session_uuid>/; reuse it via --resume.
Do NOT add authentication, HTTPS, or API keys — service binds 127.0.0.1:8421 only; remote access is Tailscale's job, not this service's.
Do NOT run launchctl from inside a workflow job — workers may be containerized. The workflow writes the plist + install script; host-side execution of `launchctl load` is a final manual step.
Do NOT pin new Python dependencies — fastapi + uvicorn are already in the repo. If they aren't, fail loudly; don't invent a pyproject edit.
Do NOT write .md design docs, README, or CHANGELOG entries — working code only.
Do NOT add feature flags, multi-user guards, or rate-limit fallbacks — single-user localhost primitive.

jobs.label: scaffold_agent_sessions_service
jobs.description: FastAPI agent_sessions.py with full route surface
jobs.outcome_goal: Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/agent_sessions.py defines `app` (FastAPI) and `__main__` that runs uvicorn on 127.0.0.1:8421; every route listed in the spec outcome_goal is implemented; per-agent asyncio.Lock prevents concurrent turns (409 on collision); transcripts appended to artifacts/agents/<agent_id>/messages.jsonl one JSON line per stream-json event.
jobs.prompt:
Build Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/agent_sessions.py. Single file. FastAPI app bound to 127.0.0.1:8421 via uvicorn in `if __name__ == '__main__'`.

Routes (all JSON in/out except /stream which is text/event-stream):

- POST /agents — body {title?: str, prompt?: str}. Mint agent_id=uuid4(). Mint session_id=uuid4(). Persist agent record {agent_id, session_id, title, created_at, last_activity} to artifacts/agents/<agent_id>/meta.json. If prompt provided, immediately run the first turn (see below). Return {agent_id, session_id, title}.

- POST /agents/{agent_id}/messages — body {prompt: str}. Acquire per-agent asyncio.Lock (return 409 if held). Shell out: `claude -p --resume <session_id> --output-format stream-json <prompt>` with cwd='/Users/nate/Praxis', env inherited, stdin closed. Parse stream-json line-by-line. For each event: append JSON line to artifacts/agents/<agent_id>/messages.jsonl AND publish to an in-memory asyncio.Queue keyed by agent_id (for /stream consumers). When subprocess exits, extract final assistant reply text and return {reply: str, turn_events: list[dict], exit_code: int}.

- GET /agents/{agent_id}/messages — read messages.jsonl, return {events: [...]}. 

- GET /agents/{agent_id}/stream — text/event-stream. Yield SSE `data: <json>\n\n` for each event as it lands in the asyncio.Queue. Close when no turn in flight for 5s OR client disconnects. EventSource-friendly format.

- DELETE /agents/{agent_id} — if a turn is in flight, kill the subprocess (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 2s). Append {event: 'terminated', at: now} to messages.jsonl. Do NOT delete artifacts.

- GET /agents — list meta.json files under artifacts/agents/, return [{agent_id, title, last_activity, running: bool}].

Implementation notes:
- Use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec for claude.
- One asyncio.Lock per agent_id, stored in a module-level dict.
- One asyncio.Queue per agent_id for live streaming, created lazily.
- When updating last_activity, rewrite meta.json atomically (write to .tmp then os.replace).
- Do not import from storage/runtime — this is a standalone surface, only uses stdlib + fastapi/uvicorn.
- No logging framework setup; just `print(..., flush=True)` to stdout/stderr for launchd to capture.

Verify locally by starting the service in background and curling /agents — must return []. Kill after.
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from surfaces.api import agent_sessions; assert hasattr(agent_sessions, "app"), "no app"; print("import ok")'

jobs.label: launchd_keepalive_assets
jobs.outcome_goal: scripts/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist defines a KeepAlive=true LaunchAgent running the service on 127.0.0.1:8421; scripts/install_agent_sessions.sh copies the plist to ~/Library/LaunchAgents, reloads launchd, and waits for the service to answer.
jobs.prompt:
Write two files. Match the style of scripts/com.praxis.authority-memory-refresh.plist and scripts/public_branch_sync.sh.

1. scripts/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist — Label=com.praxis.agent-sessions, ProgramArguments=['/opt/homebrew/bin/python3', '/Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/agent_sessions.py'], WorkingDirectory=/Users/nate/Praxis, EnvironmentVariables.PYTHONPATH='/Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow', KeepAlive=true, RunAtLoad=true, StandardOutPath=/Users/nate/Praxis/artifacts/agent_sessions.log, StandardErrorPath=/Users/nate/Praxis/artifacts/agent_sessions.err. Use & escaping where needed (Code&DBs).

2. scripts/install_agent_sessions.sh (chmod +x) — bash set -euo pipefail. Copy plist to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/. `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist 2>/dev/null || true`. `launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist`. Then poll curl http://127.0.0.1:8421/agents for up to 10s; print success with the port, or fail with diagnostic pointing at artifacts/agent_sessions.err.

Do NOT invoke launchctl from this job — workers may not have host launchctl access. The install script exists for the user to run once on-host.
jobs.verify_command: test -f scripts/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist && grep -q '<key>KeepAlive</key>' scripts/com.praxis.agent-sessions.plist && test -x scripts/install_agent_sessions.sh

jobs.label: cli_helpers
jobs.outcome_goal: scripts/praxis-agent supports subcommands: new [--title T] <prompt>, send <agent_id> <prompt>, tail <agent_id>, list.
jobs.prompt:
Write scripts/praxis-agent (chmod +x). Bash, set -euo pipefail. Dependencies: curl, jq. Base URL http://127.0.0.1:8421.

Subcommands:
- `new [--title T] <prompt>` — POST /agents; print agent_id; then stream first turn via /stream.
- `send <agent_id> <prompt>` — POST /agents/{id}/messages; print reply text (from .reply) then newline.
- `tail <agent_id>` — curl -N /agents/{id}/stream, pretty-print each SSE `data:` line with jq.
- `list` — GET /agents, tabulate with jq: agent_id, title, last_activity, running.

Usage text on no-arg or unknown subcommand, exit 2. HTTP errors exit 1. Success exit 0. Match the shell style of scripts/public_branch_sync.sh.

jobs.label: smoke_test
jobs.description: End-to-end smoke test — spawn, send, assert reply contains /Users/nate/Praxis
jobs.outcome_goal: scripts/smoke_test_agent_sessions.sh exits 0. Output log at artifacts/agent_sessions_smoke_20260420.log shows a successful turn with a reply containing '/Users/nate/Praxis'.
jobs.prompt:
Write scripts/smoke_test_agent_sessions.sh (chmod +x). Steps:

1. If nothing listening on 127.0.0.1:8421, start agent_sessions.py in background (PYTHONPATH set), remember the pid. Poll curl /agents for up to 10s.
2. POST /agents with {prompt: 'smoke: what directory are you currently running in?'}. Capture agent_id.
3. Poll GET /agents/{id}/messages for up to 60s, looking for the final assistant message.
4. Assert the assistant text contains '/Users/nate/Praxis'. Fail loudly with diagnostic if not.
5. DELETE /agents/{id}. If we started the server, kill the pid.
6. Tee all output to artifacts/agent_sessions_smoke_20260420.log.

Then run the smoke test. If claude CLI is not available in the worker's PATH, document that in the log and exit non-zero (caller will know this must be run on-host).
